lago-atitlan.title: Lake Atitlán
lago-atitlan.summary: A paradise surrounded by volcanoes in Guatemala
lago-atitlan.fullDescription: Lake Atitlán is one of the most impressive destinations in Central America, surrounded by volcanoes and Mayan villages full of color and tradition.
highlights: Indigenous Mayan villages full of culture

coban.title: Cobán
coban.summary: Exuberant nature and living culture in Alta Verapaz
coban.fullDescription: Delve into the green heart of Guatemala, where the mist kisses the mountains and the crystal clear waters of Semuc Champey invite you to adventure.
highlights: Semuc Champey and its turquoise pools

antigua-guatemala.title: Antigua Guatemala
antigua-guatemala.fullDescription: Walk the streets of one of the most beautiful colonial cities in the world, surrounded by volcanoes and full of history in every corner.
highlights: Santa Catalina Arch and cobblestone streets

san-marcos.title: San Marcos La Laguna
san-marcos.summary: Wellness, nature, and a quieter lake rhythm
san-marcos.fullDescription: San Marcos is the lake's most wellness-focused town: yoga, meditation, cacao, therapies, and calm cafes close to the dock. It also works well for swimming, walking, and slowing down, especially around Cerro Tzankujil. It is best for travelers looking for quiet, nature, and retreat energy, not nightlife.
highlights: Cerro Tzankujil: swimming, rocks, and lake views
Wellness: yoga, meditation, and cacao ceremonies
Vibe: quiet nights and retreat energy
Movement: compact village and easy walking
Best for: slowing down, swimming, and day retreats
activities: Take a yoga class with lake views
Swim or jump into the lake at Cerro Tzankujil
Join a meditation or cacao ceremony
Walk quiet trails near the village
Kayak early or around sunset
Visit vegetarian cafes and wellness spaces
Walk or take a tuk-tuk toward Tzununa
Book Indian Nose with a local guide if you want sunrise

times: To Pana or San Pedro every 30 min (6:30 AM - 6:30 PM). Price: Q25/Q15.
Short 10 min ride. Price: Q10 per person.
Safe lakeside trail, about 45 min with incredible views.

san-pedro.title: San Pedro La Laguna
san-pedro.fullDescription: San Pedro is a practical base for exploring Atitlan: volcano hikes, boats, Spanish schools, local coffee, and food near the docks. The town combines local Tz'utujil life in the upper area with a more traveler-focused lakefront zone. In the afternoon, La Finca and Playa La Finca are good options if you want a quieter side of the lake.
highlights: San Pedro Volcano: best early and with a local guide
La Finca and Playa La Finca for a quieter lake spot
Two docks: main for Panajachel and secondary for Santiago
Food: Pita Sabij, Al Tempo, and cafes near the docks
Local zone: market, shops, and services in the upper town
activities: Hike San Pedro Volcano with a local guide
Walk or take a tuk-tuk toward La Finca and Playa La Finca
Kayak early or take a short lake boat ride
Eat at Pita Sabij or try bread and pizza at Al Tempo
Drink local coffee at Cafe Las Cristalinas or Cafe Atitlan
Go out at night around Bar Sublime
Study Spanish at local schools
Visit the market and the upper part of town
Connect to San Juan, Santiago, and Panajachel from the right dock

times: Depart every 20-30 min from the main dock, usually 6:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Approx. price: Q25-35.
Depart from the secondary dock. Usually direct every hour or via nearby villages. Last boats are commonly around 4:30 PM.
Approx. 10 min ride. Typical price: Q10-15 per person. Confirm locally before riding.
Common departures are early morning, mid-morning, and afternoon, often around 5:00 AM, 9:00-9:30 AM, and 2:00-2:30 PM. Confirm availability before booking.

essentials: Pharmacies available around the town center
Local laundries near the tourist area
Basic grocery stores and small supermarkets
Local health center for basic medical attention
Carry cash, especially for boats, tuk-tuks, and small shops

san-juan.title: San Juan La Laguna
san-juan.fullDescription: San Juan is one of the best organized towns for cultural tourism on Atitlan. It is known for weaving cooperatives with natural dyes, murals, galleries, local coffee, and clean streets that are easy to explore slowly. It works very well as a half-day trip from San Pedro or as a calmer stop for buying crafts with more context.
highlights: Murals: colorful streets and local galleries
Mirador Kaqasiiwaan: short climb with lake views
Coffee: farms, roasters, and local tastings
Best for: culture, shopping, and slow exploring
activities: Visit weaving cooperatives and natural dye workshops
Walk through the murals and main streets
Climb to Mirador Kaqasiiwaan
Take a coffee tour or local tasting
Visit galleries and artisan workshops
Buy textiles directly from cooperatives
Move by tuk-tuk from San Pedro
Return by boat or walk if you have time

times: Fast and fun (10 min). Price: Q10-15.
To Pana every 30-45 min. Price: Q25.
Pleasant 30-40 min walk along the paved road.

santiago.title: Santiago Atitlán
santiago.fullDescription: Santiago Atitlan is one of the lake's strongest cultural towns. A visit usually centers on the market, Santiago Apostol Church, local brotherhoods, and Maximon, always with respect and ideally with a local guide. It is a powerful stop for understanding Tz'utujil life, trying local food, and seeing a less tourist-focused side of Atitlan.
highlights: Maximon: visit respectfully with local guidance
Market: textiles, food, and everyday life
Santiago Apostol Church: history and community
Boats: connections to San Pedro and Panajachel
Best for: culture, local food, and living history
activities: Explore the municipal market in the morning
Visit Santiago Apostol Church
Visit Maximon with a local guide
Learn about brotherhoods and community traditions
Try local food near the market
Buy textiles and local crafts
Walk the center slowly and respectfully
Arrive by boat from San Pedro's secondary dock

times: Direct (30 min) every hour. Last boat around 5:00 PM. Price: Q25.
Frequent departures from the dock near the market. Price: Q25.
Depart from the central park. Connection to the south coast.

panajachel.title: Panajachel
panajachel.summary: Gateway town, services, and boat connections
panajachel.fullDescription: Panajachel, or Pana, is the most practical entry and exit point for Lake Atitlan. It has docks connecting to other towns, shuttles, banks, pharmacies, agencies, and Santander Street for shopping, food, and tours. It is not always the quietest town, but it is the best place for logistics, first nights, and solving travel needs.
highlights: Transport: strong hub for boats and shuttles
Santander Street: shops, food, and agencies
Best for: arrival, logistics, and shopping
activities: Walk along Santander Street
Shop for crafts and solve travel errands
Take boats to other lake towns
Visit Atitlan Nature Reserve
Kayak or take a sunset boat ride
Book shuttles to Antigua or Guatemala City
Eat near Santander Street
Use Pana as your first or last night at the lake

times: Depart every 20-30 min to all villages (6:30 AM - 7:30 PM). Price: Q25 foreigners / Q10 locals.
5:30 AM, 8:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 4:00 PM. Price: Q15-25. Book at agencies on Santander Street.
Frequent departures from the main stop. Route: Pana -> Solola -> Los Encuentros -> final destination. Price: ~Q50 total.

santa-cruz.title: Santa Cruz La Laguna
santa-cruz.summary: Wide lake views, calm water, and the walk to Jaibalito
santa-cruz.fullDescription: Santa Cruz is a quiet, scenic option reached mainly by boat. The lakefront has stays, restaurants, and water activities, while the local village sits higher up the mountain. It is ideal for resting, swimming, kayaking, walking toward Jaibalito, and enjoying wide views without the pace of San Pedro or Panajachel.
highlights: Lakefront: stays, docks, and quiet swim spots
Diving: high-altitude diving school on Atitlan
activities: Swim, kayak, or paddleboard
Try high-altitude lake diving
Walk toward Jaibalito along the shore
Go up to the village and visit CECAP
Have lunch at lakefront restaurants
Rest at stays with lake views
Move by boat to Pana, San Marcos, or San Pedro
Bring cash because reliable ATMs are not available

times: Very frequent (every 20 min). Price: Q10 (local/short fare). Last boat 7:00 PM.
Pass every 30 min heading west. Wave from the dock. Price: Q20-25.

tour-sm-001.title: Yoga and Meditation Retreat
tour-sm-001.summary: A transformative mind-body connection experience in a unique natural setting.
tour-sm-001.description: Immerse yourself in the practice of yoga and meditation in one of the most energetic places on Lake Atitlán. This retreat includes sunrise yoga sessions, guided meditations, self-knowledge workshops and time for personal reflection.

pana-market-tour.title: Local Market and Santander Street Tour
pana-market-tour.summary: Explore the bustling local market and the famous Santander Street in Panajachel.
pana-market-tour.description: Immerse yourself in local life with this tour that will take you through the authentic market of Panajachel, where you can find everything from exotic fruits to traditional crafts. Then, walk down the iconic Santander Street, full of shops, galleries, and restaurants with a view of the lake.

itinerary.title: Meeting at the Central Park
itinerary.description: Brief introduction to the history of Panajachel.

itinerary.title: Local Market
itinerary.description: Tour of the fruit, vegetable, and craft stalls.

itinerary.title: Santander Street
itinerary.description: Walk along the main street of shops and restaurants.

itinerary.title: Free Time
itinerary.description: Time for shopping or exploring on your own.

pana-kayak-sunset.title: Sunset Kayak on the Lake
pana-kayak-sunset.summary: A unique experience kayaking on the waters of Lake Atitlán at sunset.
pana-kayak-sunset.description: Enjoy the breathtaking views of the volcanoes as you kayak on the calm waters of the lake. This experience includes a stop at a private beach to enjoy a snack and drinks as the sun sets behind the volcanoes.

itinerary.title: Meeting at the beach
itinerary.description: Brief instruction on safety and paddling techniques.

itinerary.title: Kayaking
itinerary.description: Tour along the coast with stops for photos.

itinerary.title: Sunset
itinerary.description: Stop at a private beach to enjoy the sunset.

itinerary.title: Return
itinerary.description: Return to Panajachel beach.

pana-culinary-tour.title: Panajachel Culinary Tour
pana-culinary-tour.summary: Discover the authentic flavors of Guatemalan cuisine on a gastronomic tour.
pana-culinary-tour.description: This tour will take you to taste the most representative dishes of Guatemalan gastronomy in the local restaurants and stalls of Panajachel. From the typical chuchitos and tamales to traditional desserts, accompanied by local drinks such as atol de elote and Guatemalan coffee.

itinerary.title: Meeting
itinerary.description: Presentation of the tour and brief introduction to Guatemalan gastronomy.

itinerary.title: Typical breakfast
itinerary.description: Tasting of traditional breakfasts.

itinerary.title: Street food
itinerary.description: Tasting of local snacks and fast food.

itinerary.title: Desserts and coffee
itinerary.description: Closing with traditional desserts and coffee tasting.

amanecer-indian-nose-rostro-maya.title: Indian Nose Sunrise (Mayan Face)
amanecer-indian-nose-rostro-maya.summary: A short pre-dawn hike to watch the sun rise over Lake Atitlán.
amanecer-indian-nose-rostro-maya.description: Climb the iconic Indian Nose viewpoint for a memorable sunrise over Lake Atitlán and the volcanoes of San Pedro, Tolimán, and Atitlán. The tour includes early pickup, a local guide, park access, and a hot drink at the summit.
amanecer-indian-nose-rostro-maya.meetingPoint: Pickup in San Pedro La Laguna

itinerary.title: Pickup
itinerary.description: Pickup at your lodging in San Pedro La Laguna.

itinerary.title: Trailhead
itinerary.description: Short briefing and start of the hike.

itinerary.title: Sunrise at the viewpoint
itinerary.description: Enjoy the sunrise with hot coffee or tea.

itinerary.title: Return
itinerary.description: Descend and return to San Pedro.

faqs.question: Is it suitable for children?
faqs.answer: Yes! The trail is short and very safe for families.

faqs.question: Do I need to be an athlete?
faqs.answer: No, it’s an easy 30-minute walk at a relaxed pace.

cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.title: Free Riders Horseback Volcano View Trail Adventure
cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.name: Free Riders Horseback Volcano View Trail Adventure
cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.summary: A panoramic horseback ride in San Pedro with group, split-private, and private options from Q350, lasting roughly 1.5 to 3 hours.
cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.description: Book your ride with Free Riders Guild and Rancho Moises to experience San Pedro on horseback through rural roads, mountain scenery, and open views over Lake Atitlan. Choose a basic prescheduled group ride from Q350, a split-private or extended ride from Q500 per person with a 2-person minimum, or a private ride from Q1000. All ride formats include basic instruction and are open to all levels.
cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.groupLabel: From 1 guest · some options require at least 2

bookingOptions.name: Basic group ride
bookingOptions.description: Shared scheduled ride, usually on Tuesdays and Thursdays and sometimes Saturdays. Starts at 10:00 am, lasts about 1.5 to 2 hours, and works best for riders with basic experience.

bookingOptions.name: Extended / split-private ride
bookingOptions.description: A more flexible option for couples or small groups. It can be scheduled at different times based on availability and usually runs longer than the basic ride.
bookingOptions.durationLabel: Variable duration depending on route and availability

bookingOptions.name: Private ride
bookingOptions.description: Private experience with flexible scheduling based on availability and dedicated coordination for your group.
bookingOptions.durationLabel: Variable duration depending on the private experience

cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.meetingPoint: Meetup to be confirmed between San Pedro Dock / Crown Coffee or Rancho Moises
cabalgata-panoramica-san-pedro.meeting_point: Meetup to be confirmed between San Pedro Dock / Crown Coffee or Rancho Moises
includes: Panoramic horseback route with Free Riders Guild and Rancho Moises
Scenic route with lake, volcano, and mountain views
highlights: Views of the lake, volcanoes, and mountains around San Pedro
All ride formats include basic instruction and are open to all levels
Full photo and video add-on available for Q500

itinerary.title: Meetup in San Pedro
itinerary.description: Meet at San Pedro Dock / Crown Coffee or receive confirmation to head directly to Rancho Moises.

itinerary.title: Instruction and departure
itinerary.description: Basic beginner instruction and preparation before starting the ride.

itinerary.title: Panoramic trail ride
itinerary.description: Ride through rural routes with open views of the lake, mountains, and volcanoes.

itinerary.title: Ride ends
itinerary.description: End of the experience and return to the agreed point.

faqs.question: Do I need previous riding experience?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. This ride welcomes beginners and more experienced riders, with basic instruction before departure.

faqs.question: What pricing options are available?
faqs.answer: The basic prescheduled group ride starts at Q350 per person. The split-private or extended option starts at Q500 per person with a 2-person minimum. The private ride starts at Q1000.

faqs.question: What is the main difference between the options?
faqs.answer: The basic option is a fixed group departure, usually Tuesdays and Thursdays at 10:00 am, and lasts around 1.5 to 2 hours. The split-private and private options can be scheduled at other times based on availability and may run longer.

faqs.question: Are there any add-ons or special packages?
faqs.answer: Yes. A full photo and video add-on is available for an extra Q500. Wedding, modeling, and special event packages are also available starting from Q5000.
